project_type: Windows desktop GUI (Python + CustomTkinter) for an existing PowerShell-based encrypted cloud storage system
build_system: pip for dependencies, PyInstaller for .exe compilation
test_framework: Python syntax validation (ast module) since we cannot install packages in this environment
build_command: python3 -m py_compile gui/app.py (syntax check only - full build requires Windows with pip install)
test_command: python3 -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('gui/app.py').read())" for each .py file
verification_instructions: 1. Validate all Python files with ast.parse for syntax correctness. 2. Verify file structure matches the plan. 3. Check that all PowerShell script references point to correct paths.
setup_instructions: On Windows: cd CloudVault && pip install -r gui/requirements.txt && python gui/app.py
environment_constraints: INTEGRATIONS_ONLY network - cannot install pip packages or pull Docker images. All code must be written and validated via AST/syntax checks only. The GUI is for Windows - uses CustomTkinter, pystray, Pillow which are not available in this sandbox.
contribution_requirements: Project uses PowerShell scripts in scripts/ directory. GUI must call these scripts via subprocess. Settings stored in config/settings.json. Logs in logs/ directory.
key_patterns: Scripts use common.ps1 library functions. Settings read from config/settings.json. Health checks via lib/health.ps1. Logging via lib/logger.ps1. Docker containers named cloudvault_*. Status script supports -Json flag for machine-readable output.
directory_structure: Root has setup.ps1, config/ (settings.json, docker-compose.yml, .env.template), scripts/ (PowerShell scripts with lib/ subfolder), docs/ (Spanish documentation). GUI will be added as gui/ directory at root level.
